perf.regime: pure-torch oracle AND the standalone implementation: out = (x.float() + residual.float()).to(dtype). A separate Triton kernel for x+residual would LOSE to torch.add (launch overhead > the add), so the reference is what a standalone call runs. The op's value is the contract a fused/persistent card references as an on-chip stage — not a kernel to launch.

notes: Deliberately reference-only: no triton/cuda card. A bare residual add is torch.add; emitting a kernel for it violates 'compose over generate' (library.md §1.2). The Op Spec exists so a persistent-grid megakernel can name the residual step as a first-class on-chip stage with a defined precision path (megakernel-blockers.md (b) point 4). verify_parity is inconclusive (single backend) — honest, not a gap.
